programmes.show_description: AC Cobra/Volvo F8T8: The team have a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck.
Ford Cortina/GT Savage Estate: Derek travels to the Yorkshire coast to pick up a 1963 Austin J2 Paralanian campervan described as the Rolls Royce of campervans.
Capri/Alpine/Lancia Fulvia Zagato: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days but Derek's still impressed. S9
Narrated by David Harewood, this documentary follows the story of the history-making 761st tank battalion composed of African-American servicemen.
Shopping from Home.
Cromer to Cambridge: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way.
Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill: Michael Portillo explores Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the town's community spirit.
Croydon to Shoreham-by-Sea: Michael Portillo travels from Croydon to Shoreham-by-Sea, hearing the story of a once-celebrated composer and visiting the oldest licensed airport in the UK.
Chichester to Cowes: Michael Portillo visits places of interest in West Sussex and Hampshire, including Southsea's King's Theatre.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Caterham 7: A unique visit behind the scenes with the small team in Dartford who hand-build the iconic open-top, two-seater Caterham 7 sports car.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: BAC, Mono R: A behind-the-scenes look at how Liverpool supercar manufacturer Briggs Automotive Company make their 270,000-pound BAC Mono R.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Land Rover Defender: We follow the 35-hour build of a new Defender, where over 3300 individual parts and 70 years of innovation come together to create a vehicle for any terrain.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Mercedes S Class: Factory 56 opens its doors as we follow the ultimate configuration of a Mercedes-Benz S-Class, from the chassis build to the main assembly floor.
Arousing Suspicions: British sauce and French farce. Rene and company must disguise themselves as Spanish dancers to recover some battle equipment from the chateau.
A Woman Never Lies: Farcical wartime sitcom. Still searching for the Fallen Madonna, Herr Flick gives Helga a truth drug.
Hitler's Last Heil: More sauce and farce. There's something to celebrate at Rene's cafe. It's his birthday and he gets a series of surprises, but not all are pleasant.
Cowley to Hunton Bridge: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legend's home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat.
Hunton Bridge to Aylesbury: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock.
Dom tries out water dowsing so he can fill his new pond. He and engineer friend Sam Lovegrove fix some church bells and then put a surf lifesaving club's quad bike back on the beach.

programmes.show_name: Tropic of Capricorn with Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Namibia and Botswana: Simon Reeve embarks on a quest to circumnavigate the globe, tracing the path of the southern tropic. The first leg sees him cross Namibia and Botswana.

programmes.show_name: Tropic of Capricorn with Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: South Africa to Madagascar: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar.

programmes.show_name: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motorcar
programmes.show_description: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car company's plant as work gets underway on a range of 4x4s, from the factory floor to the world's most exclusive car showroom.
Austin A30 Seven/Hillman Imp: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.
Land Rover 90 / Caravan: The team, including Andy Joynson, buy the right kind of Land Rover, but it's got the wrong engine. Can they clean up its emissions with a modern motor?
Austin Metro/Fiat 850 Coupe: A collection of Austin Mini Metros arrive for auction. Also under the hammer is a baby Fiat 850 coupe and a haulier offloads part of his vintage vehicle collection.
Merc G-Wagon/Opel Manta: There's an unlikely arrival at the North Yorkshire car auction - an Argentinian Army Mercedes G wagon that saw action in the Falklands War.
Escort Cosworth/Daimler: Derek has three scooters to collect, while also finding room for a Daimler and a teardrop caravan.
DBS/Ford Escort RS2000: Derek's on his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest-priced item at his next auction, but the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage.
Ford Ranchero / Thames Truck: Derek has learnt to expect the unexpected over the years, so it's not a surprise when Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi drops off his Bedford CA campervan.
Shopping from Home.
Swanwick to Brownsea Island: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century.
Whitland to Swansea: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall.
Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale Town: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales, visiting the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park.
Newport to Clevedon: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal.
Austin A30 Seven/Hillman Imp: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.
Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way.
Ellesmere Port to Audlem: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed on the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem in Cheshire.
Market Drayton to Stourport-on-Severn: Robbie gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire.
Stourport Basins to Kingswood Junction: Robbie navigates the mighty River Severn and takes an unexpected bath as he takes a tumble at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire.
Cowley to Hunton Bridge: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legend's home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat.
Hunton Bridge to Aylesbury: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock.
The actor explores Mundoo Island, which has provided a way of life for five generations of one farming family, and also investigates Phillip Island.
New. Martin heads to the lush and tropical islands of Hawaii for a look beyond its image as a tourist paradise, and into the havoc wreaked by advancing lava fields and smoking craters.

programmes.show_name: This World: Cuba With Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Journalist Simon Reeve heads to Cuba to find a communist country in the middle of a capitalist revolution and asks if the new economic openness could lead to political liberalisation.
The Nile: The adventurer follows the Nile from the Ethiopian highlands to the Mediterranean Sea, and explains how the river forged some of our earliest civilisations.

Dom tries out water dowsing so he can fill his new pond. He and engineer friend Sam Lovegrove fix some church bells and then put a surf lifesaving club's quad bike back on the beach.
Awful Wedded Wife: Farcical comedy amongst the French Resistance. Renee has organised a secret wedding with Yvette, but things have a habit of not going quite according to plan.
Firing Squashed: Wartime sitcom. Time for a final cigarette as Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen prepare to meet their maker in front of a firing squad.
A Fishful of Francs: Wartime laughs as Michelle plans to swap an eel containing the Fallen Madonna for a dogfish containing ten million francs. Well you would, wouldn't you?
